name: Executive Car Svc
recent_news: Ecslimo.com domain redirects (301) to ECS Nationwide / ECS Transportation Group, a national chauffeured ground transportation network operating in 600+ cities; indicates this SF entity is part of a broader chauffeured-service brand, not a standalone school operator.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. This is an executive/limousine/black car service (Ecslimo) tied into ECS Nationwide, not a school bus, SPED, or student transportation contractor. 4 employees, ~$352K revenue — out of scope.

name: Imperial Limousine Svc
recent_news: Founded 1977 by Monty Bruckman; specialty VIP service using restored 1940s-50s Chrysler Imperial glass limousines. No school district contracts identified. Yelp lists 'Imperial Classic Limousine Service' as closed as of Dec 2024 at the Edgewood NM location; main Albuquerque entity remains listed.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Niche vintage-limousine wedding/event service, not student transportation. No school or special-needs contracts; founder is aging (in business since 1977) but the operating model has nothing in common with Summit's tuck-in thesis.

name: Jenda Transport Inc
districts_or_customers: Hamburg NY area medical/wheelchair clients (Erie County)
recent_news: Operates as Jenda Wheelchair Transport (BBB-listed); long-tenured owner-operator (since 1970 per company record). Two BBB-listed locations. Fisher Bus Service is the dominant school-bus operator in Hamburg/Orchard Park CSDs — Jenda does NOT appear to hold school SPED contracts.
fit_notes: Marginal/no fit. Tiny (~2 employees, ~$143K revenue) NEMT/wheelchair operator, not a school bus contractor. Founder Bodekor with 55+ years of tenure = strong succession signal, but business is sub-scale and adjacent-market (medical), not Summit's core school-bus tuck-in profile.

name: LCP Transportation LLC
recent_news: ~$19.2M reported revenue (2025 per Crunchbase/RocketReach). Operates affiliate TCS LLC (a LCP Transportation Company; USDOT 2595568). NMSDC-certified (2021). No PE acquisition identified; remains owner-led by founder Ron Robinson. Materially larger than the ~$77K placeholder revenue in the source file (employee count of 1 is also wrong).
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit's core thesis. LCP is a NEMT/Medicaid transportation broker and dispatch-software operator, not a school-bus contractor with yellow-bus fleet/district contracts. Adjacent market only; would be a strategy pivot, not a tuck-in.

name: Durrin Inc
districts_or_customers: Argyle CSD area and surrounding Washington County NY school districts (Argyle, Salem, Greenwich vicinity)
recent_news: Family-owned ~45 years per company website (founded c.1980). NYSDOT Bus Operator Profiles show Durrin Inc operating ~87 buses in the 2025-26 cycle (Region 1) with a 3.45% OOS rate; 100 buses with 5.00% violation rate in 2023-24 cycle. Listed on NYSDOT Preferred/Performance-Based Bus Safety Program rolls. FMCSA-registered since Feb 2004 (USDOT 1212989). No acquisition or PE ownership identified.
fit_notes: Strong Summit tuck-in candidate. Genuine independent family-owned NY school bus operator with ~87-100 buses in upstate NY (Washington County), 45-year history under William Durrin, on NYSDOT Preferred/Good Operator list. Solid succession signal (long-tenured family CEO, no visible second generation in published materials). Geographic expansion play (NY is a Summit expansion_state); meaningful scale for a tuck-in at this revenue tier (source file's $123K rev est is clearly understated).
